name: claude-skills
description: Claude Skills meta-skill: extract domain material (docs/APIs/code/specs) into a reusable Skill (SKILL.md + references/scripts/assets), and refactor existing Skills for clarity, activation reliability, and quality gates.
capabilities: Generate skill directory structure from minimal or complete templates
Validate SKILL.md YAML frontmatter (name, description format)
Check markdown structure for required sections (When to Use, Boundaries, Quick Reference, Examples)
Enforce quality gate requirements with --strict mode
Refactor existing skills to reduce misfires and improve reliability
Extract reusable patterns from domain documentation
author: 2025Emma
securitySummary: Pure documentation meta-skill for scaffolding and validating Claude Skills. Contains two bash scripts (create-skill.sh, validate-skill.sh) that use only safe POSIX utilities. All static findings are false positives: markdown backticks for inline code formatting were misidentified as Ruby shell execution, path examples as path traversal, and documentation links as hardcoded URLs. No network access, no malicious code, no data exfiltration.
valueStatement: Building Claude Skills from domain documentation is time-consuming and error-prone. This meta-skill provides scaffolding templates, validation scripts, and a quality gate checklist so you can create production-ready skills in minutes instead of hours.
limitations: This is a meta-skill for building skills, not a domain skill itself
Cannot invent external facts; requires source material to reference
Validator is heuristic-based and assumes recommended section headings
Does not execute or test user code; only validates documentation

useCases.title: Create new skills from docs
useCases.description: Generate production-ready skill structures from API docs, specifications, or codebase READMEs in minutes.

useCases.title: Validate skill quality gates
useCases.description: Run validation scripts to ensure new skills meet naming, structure, and documentation standards before publishing.

useCases.title: Refactor existing skills
useCases.description: Fix documentation dumps, vague triggers, and missing boundaries to improve skill activation reliability.

promptTemplates.title: Create skill from docs
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the claude-skills meta-skill to create a new skill called [skill-name] from these [API docs/repository]. Include SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter, Quick Reference with 10-20 patterns, 3 reproducible examples, and references/ with index.md.

promptTemplates.title: Refactor vague skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Use claude-skills to refactor [existing-skill-path]. Apply the anti-patterns guide to fix documentation dumps, vague triggers, and missing boundaries. Run validate-skill.sh --strict when done.

promptTemplates.title: Validate skill structure
promptTemplates.prompt: Run ./skills/claude-skills/scripts/validate-skill.sh [skill-path] --strict and report any errors. Iterate until validation passes.

promptTemplates.title: Build comprehensive skill
promptTemplates.prompt: Generate a full-featured skill called [skill-name] using the complete template. Include getting_started.md, api.md, examples.md, and troubleshooting.md in references/. Use the quality checklist to ensure score >= 24.

bestPractices: Keep Quick Reference under 20 patterns with copy/paste commands; move long explanations to references/
Make frontmatter description decidable with concrete trigger keywords like 'when debugging PostgreSQL queries'
Add Not For / Boundaries section to prevent skill misfires in irrelevant contexts
antiPatterns: Documentation dumps in Quick Reference - users cannot scan or reuse paragraphs of text
Vague triggers like 'helps with databases' - activation becomes noisy and unpredictable
Missing boundaries - skill triggers in irrelevant contexts and over-promises capabilities

faq.question: What platforms support this skill?
faq.answer: Claude Skills work with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex. Check platform documentation for skill upload instructions.

faq.question: What is the minimum viable skill?
faq.answer: A minimal skill needs only SKILL.md with YAML frontmatter (name + description) and one usage example.

faq.question: How do I integrate with existing workflows?
faq.answer: Skills are activated by triggers in descriptions. Include concrete keywords and tasks that match user requests.

faq.question: Is data from this skill stored externally?
faq.answer: No. This meta-skill only creates and validates markdown files locally. No data leaves your environment.

faq.question: Validation failed with missing sections. What now?
faq.answer: Run with --strict to see required sections. Add: When to Use, Not For / Boundaries, Quick Reference, Examples, References, Maintenance.

faq.question: How does this compare to template-skill?
faq.answer: claude-skills is a production meta-skill with scaffolding, validation, and quality gates. template-skill is a basic starting point with minimal structure.
